title: Expert Curriculum Sampler Config for Diagnosis-Only SEAL/RCL
purpose: Minimal expert-oriented sampling configuration for the first loop viability smoke test.

lane_definitions.core_syndrome_reasoning: Core ID syndrome recognition across fever, respiratory, GI, CNS, skin/soft tissue, urinary, and systemic presentations.
lane_definitions.high_consequence_must_not_miss: Time-critical or high-consequence infections where vague common diagnoses should not be accepted when red flags are present.
lane_definitions.travel_tropical_vector_zoonotic: Exposure-led reasoning for returned travellers, vector-borne, tropical, zoonotic, food/water, and environmental infections.
lane_definitions.immunocompromised_or_healthcare_context: Host-context reasoning for immunocompromised, device-related, opportunistic, and healthcare-associated infections.
lane_definitions.common_infection_with_expert_trap: Common presentations enriched with discriminators that prevent generic or overbroad diagnoses.
lane_definitions.lookalike_differential_discrimination: Cases designed to distinguish close infectious lookalikes.

selection_algorithm: Filter to conditions where seal_diagnosis_task.task_inclusion == include and expert_curriculum.initial_loop_use == enabled.
Weighted-sample one training lane from active_lanes_initial.
Within the lane, prefer conditions with expert_value high or medium, then conditions with fewer saved successes.
If no prior run coverage data are available, sample uniformly within the lane.
Do not use risk_groups == general_population as a prevalence weight.
